 {"id":"frankenredis-42df","title":"Add redis-cli compatibility smoke test script","description":"# redis-cli Compatibility Smoke Test

## What
A shell script that starts FrankenRedis, runs redis-cli commands against it, and verifies outputs. This is the ultimate "drop-in replacement" proof.

## Why
If redis-cli cannot talk to FrankenRedis, it is not a drop-in replacement regardless of what internal tests say.

## Implementation
1. scripts/redis_cli_smoke_test.sh
2. Start frankenredis on a random port
3. Run redis-cli commands:
   - PING → PONG
   - SET foo bar → OK
   - GET foo → bar
   - INFO server → contains "redis_version"
   - DBSIZE → (integer) 1
   - CONFIG GET maxmemory → valid response
   - MULTI/EXEC transaction
   - EVAL "return redis.call(\"GET\",KEYS[1])" 1 foo → bar
4. Check exit code of each command
5. Clean up

## Depends On
- redis-cli must be installed (check and skip if not available)

## Files
- scripts/redis_cli_smoke_test.sh (new)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:21.374165839Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:07.348902310Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:12:07.348633631Z","close_reason":"Added scripts/redis_cli_smoke_test.sh: 25 tests covering PING, SET/GET, APPEND, EXISTS, DEL, INCR/INCRBY, RPUSH/LLEN/LPOP, HSET/HGET, SADD/SISMEMBER/SCARD, INFO, DBSIZE, EVAL, CONFIG GET. All 25/25 pass using legacy redis-cli against FrankenRedis.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-436","title":"Fix INFO replication repl_backlog_size to use runtime value","description":"## Problem

fr-command/src/lib.rs line 8271: INFO replication section hardcodes repl_backlog_size:1048576. Should read from runtime's actual repl backlog size (DEFAULT_REPL_BACKLOG_SIZE or whatever CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size has set it to).

## Implementation Plan

1. Add repl_backlog_size field to Store (synced from ServerState)
2. In INFO replication handler, read store.repl_backlog_size
3. This depends on the CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size task (frankenredis-5l2) being done first

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs: Add field
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs: Read dynamic value in INFO
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: Sync on CONFIG SET","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:23.382563695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:57:28.635192968Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:57:28.634014176Z","close_reason":"INFO replication repl_backlog_size now reads from store.server_repl_backlog_size (synced from runtime)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-436","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-5l2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:28.246670266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-436","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-wvy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:28.065781532Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-kzxw","title":"Implement ACL SAVE/LOAD file persistence","description":"# Implement ACL SAVE/LOAD file persistence

## What
ACL SAVE and ACL LOAD currently return OK without persisting. They should:
- ACL SAVE: serialize all ACL user rules to the ACL file (path from config)
- ACL LOAD: read and apply ACL rules from the ACL file, replacing current rules

## Design
Redis stores ACL rules in a text file, one user per line, in the same format as ACL SETUSER:
```
user default on ~* &* +@all
user admin on >password ~* &* +@all
user readonly on >pass ~* &* +@read -@write
```

## Implementation plan
1. Add pub acl_file_path: Option<PathBuf> to Runtime's ServerState (configured via CONFIG SET aclfile)
2. Add fn serialize_acl_rules(&self) -> String to AuthState that outputs one line per user
3. Add fn load_acl_rules(&mut self, content: &str) -> Result<(), String> to AuthState that parses lines
4. ACL SAVE: write serialize_acl_rules() to acl_file_path atomically (temp file + rename)
5. ACL LOAD: read acl_file_path, call load_acl_rules(), return OK or error
6. If acl_file_path is None, return "ERR There is no configured ACL file" (matches Redis)

## Serialization format
Each user line: `user <name> <on|off> [>password...] [~pattern...] [&pattern...] [+command|-command|+@cat|-@cat|allcommands|nocommands|allkeys|allchannels|reset]`

## Key details
- ACL LOAD replaces ALL users except "default" (which is always reset first then reconfigured)
- If parsing fails on any line, ACL LOAD rolls back to previous state and returns error
- Atomic file writes: write to .tmp then rename (already established pattern in fr-persist)

## Testing
- Unit test: serialize → load round-trip preserves all user rules
- Unit test: ACL LOAD with invalid content returns error and preserves previous state
- Conformance: ACL SAVE + ACL LOAD round-trip test

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (handle_acl_command, AuthState methods)
- crates/fr-config/src/lib.rs (if acl_file_path config is needed)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:01.991478238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:04:14.445100277Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T14:04:14.444837811Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-zjii","title":"Investigate severe throughput gap vs Redis baselines","description":"# Severe Performance Gap Against Redis Baseline

## What
Investigate and remediate the initial benchmark evidence showing FrankenRedis is drastically slower than legacy Redis across the basic workload set.

## Evidence
From `baselines/` captured on 2026-04-07:

- `SET`: FrankenRedis `1240.93 ops/sec` vs Redis `94402.47 ops/sec` (`1.31%` of Redis throughput)
- `GET`: FrankenRedis `1204.65 ops/sec` vs Redis `91142.35 ops/sec` (`1.32%` of Redis throughput)
- `MIXED`: FrankenRedis `1159.23 ops/sec` vs Redis `96834.08 ops/sec` (`1.20%` of Redis throughput)
- `INCR`: FrankenRedis `1176.59 ops/sec` vs Redis `95183.76 ops/sec` (`1.24%` of Redis throughput)
- `PIPELINE16`: FrankenRedis `1221.37 ops/sec` vs Redis `860900.42 ops/sec` (`0.14%` of Redis throughput)

Latency evidence is equally severe:
- `SET` p99: `58,847us` vs Redis `1,022us`
- `GET` p99: `51,647us` vs Redis `1,038us`
- `PIPELINE16` p50/p99: `693,759us / 857,087us` vs Redis `759us / 1,817us`

## Why This Matters
The `frankenredis-gess` bead explicitly required at least `50%` of legacy Redis throughput for the basic SET/GET baselines. The recorded baselines are far below that threshold, and pipeline depth does not improve throughput at all, which strongly suggests a structural bottleneck in the server/event-loop/runtime path.

## Investigation Targets
1. Profile FrankenRedis under the `set`, `get`, and `pipeline16` workloads.
2. Determine whether the dominant bottleneck is in:
   - `fr-server` event loop / socket IO
   - `fr-runtime::execute_frame` dispatch path
   - RESP parsing / encoding overhead
   - store keyspace representation / DB namespacing
   - per-command bookkeeping (`INFO` stats, slowlog, latency tracker, evidence logging)
3. Produce a benchmark delta report after the first optimization lever.
4. Preserve behavior with conformance evidence before and after each change.

## Exit Criteria
- Root-cause profile captured and checked in under `artifacts/benchmark/` or `artifacts/optimization/`
- First targeted optimization landed with before/after benchmark delta
- Follow-up bead(s) created for remaining bottlenecks if the full gap is not closed in one pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:29:08.439128211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:16.319272656Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:16.318401347Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
